slides.eyebrow: Behind the Scenes · How vetting works
slides.headline: 20 applications. 6 vetted.
Here's the exact checklist we use — and why we turned down 14 genuinely talented composers.
The vetting process isn't about picking the "best" music.
slides.lede: It's about picking music that's ready.

slides.headline: Because our credibility with music supervisors depends on what we pitch to them.

✓ Original compositions only (zero uncleared samples)
✓ Clear rights chain
slides.lede:
(master + publishing confirmed, documented)
✓ Lossless files available (WAV or AIFF, not MP3)
✓ ISRC codes assigned to every recording
✓ Stems available on request
✓ Sub-24-hour response time (tested in the process)

slides.headline: Of 20 applications in Month 1: 6 passed all six criteria.
The 14 who didn't?
slides.lede:
We gave each one specific written feedback.
Most gaps are fixable. They can reapply when they're ready.

slides.headline: Quality over volume.
slides.lede:
Every time.
Because supervisors need to trust what we send them.

slides.headline: Apply when you're ready: link in bio.
Open briefs. Every Tuesday.
slides.lede: Composers: apply once, get matched for life. Supervisors: shortlist in 48h.

checklist_items: Clear rights chain (master + publishing confirmed, documented)
Lossless files available (WAV or AIFF, not MP3)
ISRC codes assigned to every recording
Sub-24-hour response time (tested in the process)
